energy.headline: Easy-to-use solar panels are coming, but utilities are trying to delay them
energy.summary: Virginia is set to become the second state to pass legislation supporting plug-in solar panels, as 30+ similar bills are introduced nationwide. Utilities are raising safety concerns to delay votes.

energy.headline: Sunrun installs 48% of 2025 U.S. home battery storage
energy.summary: Sunrun installed 1.5 GWh of residential battery storage in 2025, representing nearly half the U.S. sector and controlling 44% of all cumulative residential storage deployed. The company achieved 71% battery attachment rates in Q4 2025.

energy.headline: Solar Market Insight Report 2025 Year in Review
energy.summary: U.S. installed 43 GW of solar in 2025, marking the fifth straight year as top source of new power. Residential sector ended virtually flat due to equipment shortages and tax credit deadlines.

energy.headline: Appeals court upholds California's rooftop solar rules (NEM 3.0)
energy.summary: California First Appellate District Court upheld controversial NEM 3.0 net metering rules that reduce compensation for rooftop solar owners. The decision thwarts environmental groups seeking to overturn the policy.

energy.headline: Arizona solar policy hinges on this year's Salt River Project elections
energy.summary: Landowners in SRP territory will vote between March 11-April 7 to determine solar policy at the utility serving over one-third of Arizona's electricity. Vote could profoundly affect distributed energy credits.

energy.headline: Base Power and CoServ launch 100 MW residential storage program
energy.summary: Base Power and Denton County Electric Cooperative announced a 100 MW distributed residential battery storage program across North Texas, creating one of the largest utility-led residential storage deployments.

energy.headline: Prepaid rooftop solar model targets 40% tax credits
energy.summary: SolSource Solutions and TriBeam Financial launched Propel, a prepaid third-party owned residential solar financing product that unlocks up to 40% tax credits after the federal 30% credit expired.

energy.headline: Hawaii rooftop solar incentives could be retooled toward lower-income households
energy.summary: Hawaii lawmakers may remove the $5,000 cap on the state's 35% renewable energy tax credit and set new income limits to target low and moderate-income households as federal incentives end.

energy.headline: New US battery capacity in 2026: 24.3 GW of new battery storage to come online
energy.summary: 86 GW of new U.S. utility-scale capacity slated for 2026 with 26.3 GW from battery storage. Solar and storage account for 79% of all planned additions, with residential battery attachment rates accelerating.

energy.headline: Residential battery storage industry white paper 2026
energy.summary: By 2026, residential battery storage enters a scale-up phase driven by falling lithium prices and stricter carbon goals. Payback periods have shortened to 5-8 years in mature solar markets.

energy.headline: Long-duration energy storage deployments rose 49% in 2025
energy.summary: Global long-duration energy storage exceeded 15 GWh in 2025 with 49% growth, but lithium-ion batteries are outcompeting emerging technologies. China accounts for 93% of cumulative LDES installations.

energy.headline: Pila launches Mesh Home Battery plug-in storage at $1,299
energy.summary: Pila introduced Mesh Home Battery, a portable 45-pound plug-in home battery that stores energy from grid or solar, offering affordable backup power at $1,299 versus $8,400-$14,400 for Tesla Powerwall.

energy.headline: Energy Storage is Revolutionizing America's Power Sector
energy.summary: U.S. residential battery capacity grew 51% in 2025 and is expected to grow 120% by 2030. The nation added 57 GWh of energy storage capacity in 2025 and forecasts 70 GWh in 2026.

energy.headline: Tax credits for solar panels available but requires leasing
energy.summary: After the 30% federal tax credit for purchased residential solar systems expired December 31, 2025, homeowners can still access incentives through solar leases and power purchase agreements through 2028.

energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before the tax credit cut and reshaped the market
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar before December 31, 2025 deadline, creating a 205% year-over-year surge. Battery attachment rates declined as customers prioritized solar installations over storage.

energy.headline: Rooftop solar is booming but not in the communities that need it most
energy.summary: Despite rapid solar adoption nationwide, deployment overlooks underserved communities due to median $30,000 installation costs. Current models fail to serve high-potential, low-income areas equitably.

energy.headline: Net metering policies see significant revision in states across the U.S.
energy.summary: About one-third of U.S. states are offering alternatives to traditional net metering or making significant revisions. Nevada and other states are shifting from monthly netting to 15-minute netting.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves
energy.summary: As net metering compensation rates decline in states like California, homeowners increasingly prioritize battery storage over exporting excess solar power to the grid for lower rates.

energy.headline: Solect Energy details rooftop solar site leases for property owners
energy.summary: Commercial rooftop solar site leases are emerging as a practical strategy generating long-term non-operational income for property owners through 20-25 year lease agreements with developers.

energy.headline: 43 GW: Solar tops new U.S. power for the 5th year in a row
energy.summary: U.S. installed 43 GW of solar in 2025, the fifth consecutive year as top electricity source. Solar and storage together accounted for 79% of new generation capacity despite policy uncertainty.

energy.headline: Solar News This Week - March 8, 2026
energy.summary: Utah remains the only state with legalized plug-in solar kits; 29 states are considering similar legislation. Solar will provide 51% of new utility-scale electricity capacity in 2026 according to EIA.

energy.headline: Expiring solar tax credits shine a light on benefit inequities
energy.summary: Solar tax credits disproportionately benefit upper-income households. The elimination of 30% credits will exacerbate equity issues as lower-income families cannot access remaining incentives.

energy.headline: Home electrification accelerating with integrated systems approach
energy.summary: Instead of purchasing individual products, homeowners are increasingly planning coordinated home energy systems combining solar, storage, and EV charging to control costs and improve resilience.

energy.headline: U.S. residential storage market outlook Q1 2026
energy.summary: U.S. BESS deployments are set to increase to 70 GWh/35 GW in 2026. Behind-the-meter residential market saw strong growth as the 25D tax credit ended, with 3.1 GWh installed in 2025.
